rawText:
Operation Epic Fury, the most intensive opening air campaign in modern military history -- with roughly 5,000 munitions expended in its first four days and 11,000 in its first sixteen -- was launched by an administration whose own vice-president had warned, as a senator in 2024, that America did not manufacture enough munitions to sustain simultaneous conflicts in eastern Europe, the Middle East, and East Asia, a contradiction that now threatens to sap American military power for years to come.

The campaign's initial phase consumed over 1,000 scarce and expensive "stand-off" munitions, hundreds of medium-range and anti-radiation missiles, and an estimated 140 Patriot PAC-3 MSE interceptors alongside more than 150 THAAD interceptors -- stockpiles that were already depleted after a quarter of America's THAAD inventory was fired defending Israel the previous year -- at a replacement cost of $20 billion to $26 billion for the first four days alone, a sum Congress might appropriate overnight but which cannot conjure into existence the gallium, neodymium, and ammonium perchlorate on which missile production depends.

The relentless assassination campaign against Iran's leadership, which has killed the original supreme leader, the defence minister, several generals, the security chief Ali Larijani, the intelligence minister, and the heads of the Basij paramilitary force, reflects Israel's strategy of progressively disintegrating the state -- yet it has simultaneously removed the pragmatic interlocutors most likely to negotiate an end to the war, potentially replacing them with hardliners who prefer martyrdom and are more inclined to pursue nuclear weapons.

America's gunboat diplomacy, rooted in what critics describe as a 19th-century imperial nostalgia that confuses the projection of firepower with the exercise of strategy, has repeatedly stumbled against adversaries who refuse to capitulate: just as Venezuela's Maduro did not "cry uncle" when drug-running boats were vaporised, Iran's regime -- having learned from the Houthis that low-tech drones can paralyse shipping despite high-tech naval countermeasures -- has imposed a de facto blockade on the Strait of Hormuz that no navy can easily break, given the waterway's forbidding 54-kilometre width, flanking mountains, and the seconds-long reaction window available to escort ships.

The strain on the US Navy, already stretched thin with only a handful of its eleven aircraft carriers available at any time, has become acute: the USS Gerald R. Ford has been at sea for nearly 270 days and is on course to break the longest carrier deployment record since the Vietnam War, while a 30-hour fire that left 600 sailors without beds illustrates the toll of operating, as one former Pentagon official put it, "like driving a car at 200 miles per hour for months, without an oil change."

Iran's own strategic calculus has proved more sophisticated than Washington anticipated, for the regime has waged two parallel wars simultaneously -- one defensive, absorbing air strikes it cannot repel, and one offensive, attacking shipping, energy infrastructure, and Gulf state facilities with enough drones and missiles to keep the strait closed and oil prices climbing, thereby accumulating the very economic leverage that Donald Trump, facing midterm elections and petrol prices up 34% in a month, can least afford to concede.

The war has, paradoxically, pushed America's Gulf allies closer to Washington's objectives even as it has alienated them diplomatically: Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Qatar, having borne the brunt of Iranian retaliation including a ballistic-missile strike on Qatar's LNG plant that may knock 3% of global supply offline for up to five years, have concluded that the conflict cannot end with an emboldened regime capable of holding their economies hostage, yet they lack the military capability to secure the strait themselves.

Whatever tactical advantages America has gained -- including the combat debut of cheap drones like the LUCAS system, invaluable real-world experience with AI-enabled targeting, and the systematic destruction of Iran's conventional navy -- are shadowed by the strategic intelligence being revealed to China, which is closely studying how America de-mines waterways, the time required for such operations, and the tactics employed, information that could prove decisive should Beijing decide to blockade Taiwan.

The IRGC, far from collapsing under the bombardment, has activated contingency plans to disperse into as many as 30,000 independent five-man cells capable of sustained sabotage and guerrilla operations, while designating up to three successors for each assassinated commander -- a resilience rooted not in ideological cohesion but in an organisational structure that, as one of the corps' own founders has observed, is riven by corruption, internal dissatisfaction, and a rank-and-file loyalty that extends more to Iran's territory than to the regime's survival.

The ultimate irony of Operation Epic Fury is that the officials who launched it -- J.D. Vance and his allies in Trump's orbit -- entered office arguing that America had squandered blood and treasure in post-2001 Middle Eastern wars and must husband its resources for a potential conflict with China, yet the war they have chosen to fight is cannibalising forces from Asia, diverting a marine expeditionary unit from Japan and THAAD components from South Korea, and degrading missile-defence capability in a manner that, as one analyst at CSIS warns, "may well undercut deterrence in the Pacific for the remainder of this decade."

overview.title: America's Military Gamble in the Persian Gulf

sentences.original: Operation Epic Fury, the most intensive opening air campaign in modern military history -- with roughly 5,000 munitions expended in its first four days and 11,000 in its first sixteen -- was launched by an administration whose own vice-president had warned, as a senator in 2024, that America did not manufacture enough munitions to sustain simultaneous conflicts in eastern Europe, the Middle East, and East Asia, a contradiction that now threatens to sap American military power for years to come.
sentences.translation: 에픽 퓨리 작전은 현대 군사 역사상 가장 집중적인 개전 항공 작전으로 -- 첫 4일 동안 약 5,000발, 첫 16일 동안 11,000발의 탄약이 소모되었다 -- 자기 부통령이 2024년 상원의원 시절에 미국이 동유럽, 중동, 동아시아에서 동시 분쟁을 지속할 만큼 충분한 탄약을 생산하지 못한다고 경고했던 바로 그 행정부가 개시했으며, 이 모순은 이제 앞으로 수년간 미국의 군사력을 잠식할 위험이 있다.

grammarElements.text: Operation Epic Fury
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: the most intensive opening air campaign in modern military history
grammarElements.label: 동격 삽입구

grammarElements.text: was launched by
grammarElements.label: 수동태 동사

grammarElements.text: an administration whose own vice-president had warned
grammarElements.label: 행위자 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: a contradiction that now threatens to sap American military power
grammarElements.label: 동격 관계절

vocabulary.example: The factory shifted to producing munitions after the war began.
Years of underfunding have sapped the military's readiness.

expressions.usage: 어떤 영향이 장기간 지속될 것임을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Action] was launched by an administration whose own [official] had warned that [contradictory fact]
patterns.explanation: 행동과 그 행동을 개시한 주체 내부의 모순을 대비시키는 아이러니 패턴
examples: The trade war was launched by an administration whose own Treasury Secretary had warned that tariffs would harm American consumers.
The deregulation was pushed by an agency whose own scientists had warned that safety standards were already too lax.

drillQuestions.question: Operation Epic Fury was launched by an administration whose own vice-president had warned that America did not manufacture enough ______ to sustain simultaneous conflicts.

sentences.notes: 기사의 핵심 아이러니를 도입부에서 즉시 제시. 작전의 규모와 그것을 개시한 행정부의 과거 경고 사이의 모순이 전체 논지를 관통한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Operation Epic Fury, the most intensive opening air campaign
koreanAlignment.ko: 현대 군사 역사상 가장 집중적인 개전 항공 작전인 에픽 퓨리 작전

koreanAlignment.en: was launched by an administration whose own vice-president had warned
koreanAlignment.ko: 자기 부통령이 경고했던 바로 그 행정부가 개시했다

koreanAlignment.en: America did not manufacture enough munitions
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국이 충분한 탄약을 생산하지 못한다

koreanAlignment.en: a contradiction that now threatens to sap American military power
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국의 군사력을 잠식할 위험이 있는 모순

sentences.original: The campaign's initial phase consumed over 1,000 scarce and expensive "stand-off" munitions, hundreds of medium-range and anti-radiation missiles, and an estimated 140 Patriot PAC-3 MSE interceptors alongside more than 150 THAAD interceptors -- stockpiles that were already depleted after a quarter of America's THAAD inventory was fired defending Israel the previous year -- at a replacement cost of $20 billion to $26 billion for the first four days alone, a sum Congress might appropriate overnight but which cannot conjure into existence the gallium, neodymium, and ammonium perchlorate on which missile production depends.
sentences.translation: 이 작전의 초기 단계에서 1,000발 이상의 희귀하고 값비싼 '스탠드오프' 탄약, 수백 발의 중거리 및 대방사 미사일, 그리고 추정 140기의 패트리어트 PAC-3 MSE 요격체와 150기 이상의 사드 요격체가 소모되었다 -- 전년도에 이스라엘 방어에 미국 사드 재고의 4분의 1이 발사된 후 이미 고갈된 비축량이었다 -- 첫 4일만의 교체 비용이 200억에서 260억 달러에 달하며, 이는 의회가 하룻밤 사이에 예산을 배정할 수 있는 금액이지만, 미사일 생산이 의존하는 갈륨, 네오디뮴, 과염소산암모늄을 무에서 만들어낼 수는 없다.

grammarElements.text: The campaign's initial phase
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: consumed
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: over 1,000 ... munitions, hundreds of ... missiles, and ... interceptors
grammarElements.label: 병렬 목적어 (세 가지)

grammarElements.text: stockpiles that were already depleted
grammarElements.label: 동격 관계절

grammarElements.text: a sum Congress might appropriate but which cannot conjure into existence
grammarElements.label: 대조 관계절

vocabulary.example: Depleted stockpiles left the alliance vulnerable to a second attack.

expressions.usage: 돈만으로는 해결할 수 없는 물리적 한계를 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Sum] Congress might appropriate overnight but which cannot conjure into existence [critical resource]
patterns.explanation: 재정적 해결과 물리적 해결의 간극을 대비하는 패턴
examples: A billion dollars Congress might approve tomorrow but which cannot conjure into existence the trained engineers the project demands.
Funding the government might allocate quickly but which cannot conjure into existence the rare earth minerals the industry requires.

drillQuestions.question: The stockpiles were already ______ after a quarter of America's THAAD inventory was fired defending Israel the previous year.

sentences.notes: 구체적 수치와 화학물질 이름의 나열이 추상적 군비 문제를 생생하게 전달. conjure into existence라는 표현이 예산과 생산의 괴리를 효과적으로 포착.

koreanAlignment.en: The campaign's initial phase consumed
koreanAlignment.ko: 작전의 초기 단계에서 소모되었다

koreanAlignment.en: scarce and expensive stand-off munitions
koreanAlignment.ko: 희귀하고 값비싼 스탠드오프 탄약

koreanAlignment.en: stockpiles that were already depleted
koreanAlignment.ko: 이미 고갈된 비축량

koreanAlignment.en: a sum Congress might appropriate overnight
koreanAlignment.ko: 의회가 하룻밤 사이에 배정할 수 있는 금액

koreanAlignment.en: cannot conjure into existence the gallium, neodymium, and ammonium perchlorate
koreanAlignment.ko: 갈륨, 네오디뮴, 과염소산암모늄을 무에서 만들어낼 수는 없다

sentences.original: The relentless assassination campaign against Iran's leadership, which has killed the original supreme leader, the defence minister, several generals, the security chief Ali Larijani, the intelligence minister, and the heads of the Basij paramilitary force, reflects Israel's strategy of progressively disintegrating the state -- yet it has simultaneously removed the pragmatic interlocutors most likely to negotiate an end to the war, potentially replacing them with hardliners who prefer martyrdom and are more inclined to pursue nuclear weapons.
sentences.translation: 이란 지도부를 겨냥한 가차 없는 암살 작전은 원래 최고지도자, 국방장관, 여러 장성, 보안 수장 알리 라리자니, 정보장관, 바시지 준군사조직 수장들을 제거했으며, 이는 국가를 점진적으로 해체하는 이스라엘의 전략을 반영한다 -- 그러나 동시에 전쟁 종결을 협상할 가능성이 가장 높았던 실용주의적 대화 상대를 제거해 버렸고, 그 자리를 순교를 선호하고 핵무기 추구에 더 기울어진 강경파로 대체할 가능성을 열어놓았다.

grammarElements.text: The relentless assassination campaign against Iran's leadership
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: reflects
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: Israel's strategy of progressively disintegrating the state
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: yet it has simultaneously removed the pragmatic interlocutors
grammarElements.label: 대조절 (yet)

vocabulary.example: Without a credible interlocutor, peace talks stalled indefinitely.

expressions.usage: 체계적으로 조직이나 국가를 약화시키는 전략을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Action] reflects [strategy A] -- yet it has simultaneously [unintended consequence B]
patterns.explanation: 전략의 의도와 그로 인한 역효과를 대비시키는 yet 반전 패턴
examples: The crackdown reflects the government's desire for order -- yet it has simultaneously radicalised the opposition.
The merger reflects a strategy of market dominance -- yet it has simultaneously alienated the company's most loyal customers.

drillQuestions.question: The assassination campaign has simultaneously removed the pragmatic ______ most likely to negotiate an end to the war.

sentences.notes: reflects와 yet의 대비가 암살 전략의 이중성을 포착한다. 전술적 성공이 전략적 실패로 이어질 수 있다는 역설이 핵심.

koreanAlignment.en: The relentless assassination campaign against Iran's leadership
koreanAlignment.ko: 이란 지도부를 겨냥한 가차 없는 암살 작전

koreanAlignment.en: reflects Israel's strategy of progressively disintegrating the state
koreanAlignment.ko: 국가를 점진적으로 해체하는 이스라엘의 전략을 반영한다

koreanAlignment.en: removed the pragmatic interlocutors most likely to negotiate
koreanAlignment.ko: 협상할 가능성이 가장 높았던 실용주의적 대화 상대를 제거했다

koreanAlignment.en: hardliners who prefer martyrdom
koreanAlignment.ko: 순교를 선호하는 강경파

sentences.original: America's gunboat diplomacy, rooted in what critics describe as a 19th-century imperial nostalgia that confuses the projection of firepower with the exercise of strategy, has repeatedly stumbled against adversaries who refuse to capitulate: just as Venezuela's Maduro did not "cry uncle" when drug-running boats were vaporised, Iran's regime -- having learned from the Houthis that low-tech drones can paralyse shipping despite high-tech naval countermeasures -- has imposed a de facto blockade on the Strait of Hormuz that no navy can easily break, given the waterway's forbidding 54-kilometre width, flanking mountains, and the seconds-long reaction window available to escort ships.
sentences.translation: 비평가들이 화력 투사와 전략 행사를 혼동하는 19세기적 제국 향수에 뿌리를 둔 것이라고 묘사하는 미국의 포함 외교는, 항복을 거부하는 적수들 앞에서 번번이 좌절했다. 마두로의 베네수엘라가 마약 운반선이 기화되었을 때 항복하지 않았던 것처럼, 이란 정권은 -- 후시 반군으로부터 저기술 드론이 첨단 해군 대응 수단에도 불구하고 해운을 마비시킬 수 있다는 것을 배운 후 -- 어떤 해군도 쉽게 깨뜨릴 수 없는 호르무즈 해협의 사실상 봉쇄를 부과했으며, 이는 54킬로미터의 험준한 수로 폭, 양쪽을 둘러싼 산맥, 호위함에 주어진 수 초의 대응 시간 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: America's gunboat diplomacy
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: rooted in what critics describe as a 19th-century imperial nostalgia
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 삽입구

grammarElements.text: has repeatedly stumbled against
grammarElements.label: 동사구

grammarElements.text: just as Venezuela's Maduro did not cry uncle
grammarElements.label: 유비 부사절

vocabulary.example: The government refused to capitulate to the rebels' demands.

expressions.usage: 군사적 위협으로 상대국을 강압하는 외교 방식을 비판적으로 지칭할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Approach], rooted in [ideological basis], has repeatedly stumbled against [adversaries who resist]
patterns.explanation: 특정 접근법의 이념적 토대와 그 반복적 실패를 연결하는 패턴
examples: The austerity programme, rooted in textbook monetarism, has repeatedly stumbled against voters who refuse to accept further cuts.
The containment strategy, rooted in Cold War assumptions, has repeatedly stumbled against non-state actors who operate outside traditional power structures.

drillQuestions.question: America's gunboat diplomacy has repeatedly stumbled against adversaries who refuse to ______.

sentences.notes: gunboat diplomacy라는 역사적 개념과 현대 비대칭전의 현실을 대비. 콜론 뒤에 베네수엘라-이란 유비가 논증을 강화한다.

koreanAlignment.en: America's gunboat diplomacy
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국의 포함 외교

koreanAlignment.en: rooted in a 19th-century imperial nostalgia
koreanAlignment.ko: 19세기적 제국 향수에 뿌리를 둔

koreanAlignment.en: has repeatedly stumbled against adversaries who refuse to capitulate
koreanAlignment.ko: 항복을 거부하는 적수들 앞에서 번번이 좌절했다

koreanAlignment.en: has imposed a de facto blockade on the Strait of Hormuz
koreanAlignment.ko: 호르무즈 해협의 사실상 봉쇄를 부과했다

sentences.original: The strain on the US Navy, already stretched thin with only a handful of its eleven aircraft carriers available at any time, has become acute: the USS Gerald R. Ford has been at sea for nearly 270 days and is on course to break the longest carrier deployment record since the Vietnam War, while a 30-hour fire that left 600 sailors without beds illustrates the toll of operating, as one former Pentagon official put it, "like driving a car at 200 miles per hour for months, without an oil change."
sentences.translation: 언제든 11척의 항공모함 중 소수만 가용한 상태로 이미 한계까지 늘어난 미 해군의 부담은 심각해졌다. USS 제럴드 R. 포드호는 거의 270일간 바다에 나가 있으며 베트남전 이래 최장 항모 배치 기록을 깰 기세이고, 600명의 수병을 침대 없이 만든 30시간짜리 화재는 한 전직 펜타곤 관리의 표현대로 '오일 교환 없이 몇 달간 시속 200마일로 차를 모는 것 같은' 운용의 대가를 보여준다.

grammarElements.text: The strain on the US Navy
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: already stretched thin with only a handful of its eleven aircraft carriers available
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 삽입구

grammarElements.text: has become acute
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 보어

grammarElements.text: as one former Pentagon official put it
grammarElements.label: 인용 삽입절

vocabulary.example: The housing shortage has become acute in major cities.

expressions.usage: 인력이나 자원이 과도하게 분산되어 여유가 없는 상태를 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [The strain on X], already [condition], has become acute: [specific evidence]
patterns.explanation: 이미 취약한 상태에서 한계를 넘어선 부담을 콜론 뒤 구체적 증거로 제시하는 패턴
examples: The strain on hospitals, already short-staffed after the pandemic, has become acute: emergency rooms are turning patients away and nurses are quitting in record numbers.
The strain on the power grid, already ageing beyond its design life, has become acute: blackouts have tripled and maintenance backlogs stretch for years.

drillQuestions.question: The strain on the US Navy, already stretched thin, has become ______: the USS Gerald R. Ford has been at sea for nearly 270 days.

sentences.notes: 콜론 뒤 구체적 수치(270일, 30시간, 600명)와 생생한 비유(오일 교환 없이 달리는 차)가 해군의 과부하를 실감나게 전달.

koreanAlignment.en: The strain on the US Navy, already stretched thin
koreanAlignment.ko: 이미 한계까지 늘어난 미 해군의 부담

koreanAlignment.en: has become acute
koreanAlignment.ko: 심각해졌다

koreanAlignment.en: is on course to break the longest carrier deployment record
koreanAlignment.ko: 최장 항모 배치 기록을 깰 기세이다

koreanAlignment.en: like driving a car at 200 miles per hour for months, without an oil change
koreanAlignment.ko: 오일 교환 없이 몇 달간 시속 200마일로 차를 모는 것 같은

sentences.original: Iran's own strategic calculus has proved more sophisticated than Washington anticipated, for the regime has waged two parallel wars simultaneously -- one defensive, absorbing air strikes it cannot repel, and one offensive, attacking shipping, energy infrastructure, and Gulf state facilities with enough drones and missiles to keep the strait closed and oil prices climbing, thereby accumulating the very economic leverage that Donald Trump, facing midterm elections and petrol prices up 34% in a month, can least afford to concede.
sentences.translation: 이란 자체의 전략적 계산은 워싱턴이 예상했던 것보다 훨씬 정교한 것으로 드러났다. 이 정권은 두 가지 전쟁을 동시에 수행해 왔기 때문이다 -- 하나는 방어전으로 막아낼 수 없는 공습을 흡수하고, 다른 하나는 공격전으로 해운, 에너지 인프라, 걸프 국가 시설을 충분한 드론과 미사일로 타격하여 해협을 폐쇄하고 유가를 계속 끌어올림으로써, 중간선거를 앞두고 한 달 만에 휘발유 가격이 34% 오른 도널드 트럼프가 가장 양보할 수 없는 바로 그 경제적 지렛대를 축적해 온 것이다.

grammarElements.text: Iran's own strategic calculus
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: has proved more sophisticated than Washington anticipated
grammarElements.label: 동사구 + 비교급 보어

grammarElements.text: for the regime has waged two parallel wars simultaneously
grammarElements.label: 이유절 (for)

grammarElements.text: thereby accumulating the very economic leverage
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문 (thereby)

vocabulary.example: The political calculus shifted after the election results came in.

expressions.usage: 어떤 것을 포기하는 것이 가장 치명적인 상황을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Actor]'s calculus has proved more sophisticated than [opponent] anticipated, for [evidence of dual strategy]
patterns.explanation: 상대의 전략이 예상보다 정교했음을 밝히고 이유를 제시하는 패턴
examples: Beijing's calculus has proved more sophisticated than Brussels anticipated, for China has simultaneously courted eastern European nations while pressuring western ones.
The union's calculus has proved more sophisticated than management anticipated, for workers have combined strikes with a social media campaign that shifted public opinion.

drillQuestions.question: Iran's own strategic ______ has proved more sophisticated than Washington anticipated.

sentences.notes: 이란의 이중 전략(방어전+공격전)을 병렬 구조로 대비시키고, thereby로 그 결과를 트럼프의 정치적 약점에 연결하는 정교한 문장.

koreanAlignment.en: Iran's own strategic calculus has proved more sophisticated than Washington anticipated
koreanAlignment.ko: 이란의 전략적 계산은 워싱턴의 예상보다 정교했다

koreanAlignment.en: the regime has waged two parallel wars simultaneously
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 정권은 두 가지 전쟁을 동시에 수행해 왔다

koreanAlignment.en: one defensive ... and one offensive
koreanAlignment.ko: 하나는 방어전 ... 다른 하나는 공격전

koreanAlignment.en: the very economic leverage that Trump can least afford to concede
koreanAlignment.ko: 트럼프가 가장 양보할 수 없는 바로 그 경제적 지렛대

sentences.original: The war has, paradoxically, pushed America's Gulf allies closer to Washington's objectives even as it has alienated them diplomatically: Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Qatar, having borne the brunt of Iranian retaliation including a ballistic-missile strike on Qatar's LNG plant that may knock 3% of global supply offline for up to five years, have concluded that the conflict cannot end with an emboldened regime capable of holding their economies hostage, yet they lack the military capability to secure the strait themselves.
sentences.translation: 역설적이게도, 이 전쟁은 미국의 걸프 동맹국들을 외교적으로 소외시키면서도 동시에 워싱턴의 목표에 더 가깝게 밀어붙였다. 카타르 LNG 공장에 대한 탄도미사일 공격을 포함한 이란 보복의 정면을 감당해 온 사우디아라비아, UAE, 카타르는 -- 최대 5년간 세계 공급의 3%를 중단시킬 수 있는 타격이었다 -- 자국 경제를 인질로 잡을 수 있는 대담해진 정권을 남겨둔 채 이 분쟁이 끝나서는 안 된다고 결론 내렸지만, 해협을 스스로 확보할 군사력은 갖추지 못하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The war
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: has, paradoxically, pushed
grammarElements.label: 동사 (부사 삽입)

grammarElements.text: closer to Washington's objectives even as it has alienated them
grammarElements.label: 보어 + 동시 대조절

grammarElements.text: yet they lack the military capability
grammarElements.label: 대조절 (yet)

vocabulary.example: The sanctions paradoxically strengthened the regime's grip on power.

expressions.usage: 한 국가가 다른 국가의 경제적 취약점을 위협 수단으로 사용하는 상황을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Event] has, paradoxically, [positive effect] even as it has [negative effect]
patterns.explanation: 하나의 사건이 동시에 상반된 두 결과를 낳는 역설을 표현하는 패턴
examples: The recession has, paradoxically, strengthened the company's market position even as it has decimated its revenue.
The scandal has, paradoxically, increased the politician's name recognition even as it has destroyed his credibility.

drillQuestions.question: The war has, ______, pushed America's Gulf allies closer to Washington's objectives even as it has alienated them diplomatically.

sentences.notes: paradoxically와 even as의 결합이 동맹 관계의 이중적 변화를 정확히 포착. 외교적 소외와 전략적 수렴이라는 모순이 핵심.

koreanAlignment.en: The war has, paradoxically, pushed allies closer to Washington's objectives
koreanAlignment.ko: 전쟁은 역설적으로 동맹국들을 워싱턴의 목표에 더 가깝게 밀어붙였다

koreanAlignment.en: even as it has alienated them diplomatically
koreanAlignment.ko: 외교적으로 소외시키면서도

koreanAlignment.en: having borne the brunt of Iranian retaliation
koreanAlignment.ko: 이란 보복의 정면을 감당해 온

koreanAlignment.en: an emboldened regime capable of holding their economies hostage
koreanAlignment.ko: 자국 경제를 인질로 잡을 수 있는 대담해진 정권

sentences.original: Whatever tactical advantages America has gained -- including the combat debut of cheap drones like the LUCAS system, invaluable real-world experience with AI-enabled targeting, and the systematic destruction of Iran's conventional navy -- are shadowed by the strategic intelligence being revealed to China, which is closely studying how America de-mines waterways, the time required for such operations, and the tactics employed, information that could prove decisive should Beijing decide to blockade Taiwan.
sentences.translation: 미국이 얻은 전술적 이점이 무엇이든 -- LUCAS 시스템 같은 저가 드론의 실전 데뷔, AI 기반 표적 지정의 귀중한 실전 경험, 이란 재래식 해군의 체계적 파괴 등 -- 그것은 중국에 노출되고 있는 전략 정보에 의해 가려진다. 중국은 미국이 어떻게 수로의 기뢰를 제거하는지, 그러한 작전에 얼마나 시간이 걸리는지, 어떤 전술이 사용되는지를 면밀히 연구하고 있으며, 이 정보는 베이징이 대만 봉쇄를 결정할 경우 결정적으로 작용할 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: Whatever tactical advantages America has gained
grammarElements.label: 양보 명사절 주어 (Whatever)

grammarElements.text: are shadowed by
grammarElements.label: 수동태 동사

grammarElements.text: the strategic intelligence being revealed to China
grammarElements.label: 행위자 (수동태의 by절)

grammarElements.text: should Beijing decide to blockade Taiwan
grammarElements.label: 도치 조건절

vocabulary.example: The team's victory was shadowed by the injury of its star player.

expressions.usage: 어떤 요소가 결과를 좌우할 만큼 중요할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Whatever [advantages gained] are shadowed by [greater risk], information that could prove decisive should [future scenario]
patterns.explanation: 전술적 이득이 전략적 위험에 의해 무색해짐을 보여주는 양보-반전 패턴
examples: Whatever market share the company has gained is shadowed by the debt it accumulated, a burden that could prove fatal should interest rates rise further.
Whatever goodwill the government has earned is shadowed by the precedent it has set, a risk that could prove devastating should a less scrupulous leader take office.

drillQuestions.question: Whatever tactical advantages America has gained are ______ by the strategic intelligence being revealed to China.

sentences.notes: Whatever ... are shadowed by 구조가 전술적 승리의 이면에 숨은 전략적 대가를 드러낸다. 대만 시나리오로의 연결이 기사의 지정학적 시야를 넓힌다.

koreanAlignment.en: Whatever tactical advantages America has gained
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국이 얻은 전술적 이점이 무엇이든

koreanAlignment.en: are shadowed by the strategic intelligence being revealed to China
koreanAlignment.ko: 중국에 노출되고 있는 전략 정보에 의해 가려진다

koreanAlignment.en: which is closely studying how America de-mines waterways
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국이 어떻게 수로의 기뢰를 제거하는지 면밀히 연구하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: should Beijing decide to blockade Taiwan
koreanAlignment.ko: 베이징이 대만 봉쇄를 결정할 경우

sentences.original: The IRGC, far from collapsing under the bombardment, has activated contingency plans to disperse into as many as 30,000 independent five-man cells capable of sustained sabotage and guerrilla operations, while designating up to three successors for each assassinated commander -- a resilience rooted not in ideological cohesion but in an organisational structure that, as one of the corps' own founders has observed, is riven by corruption, internal dissatisfaction, and a rank-and-file loyalty that extends more to Iran's territory than to the regime's survival.
sentences.translation: 이란혁명수비대(IRGC)는 폭격 아래 무너지기는커녕 비상 계획을 가동하여 최대 30,000개의 독립적 5인 조 단위로 분산해 지속적인 사보타주와 게릴라 작전을 수행할 태세를 갖추었으며, 암살된 각 지휘관마다 최대 3명의 후계자를 지정해 두었다 -- 이 회복력은 이념적 결속이 아니라, 혁명수비대 자체 창설자 중 한 명이 관찰한 바와 같이 부패, 내부 불만, 그리고 정권의 생존보다 이란 영토에 더 향하는 병사들의 충성심으로 관통된 조직 구조에 뿌리를 두고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The IRGC
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: far from collapsing under the bombardment
grammarElements.label: 양보적 분사구 (far from)

grammarElements.text: has activated contingency plans
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: a resilience rooted not in ... but in ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사 + not A but B 구조

vocabulary.example: Every military operation requires a contingency plan for withdrawal.

expressions.usage: 예상과 정반대의 결과를 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Actor], far from [expected outcome], has [actual response] -- a resilience rooted not in [obvious reason] but in [deeper reason]
patterns.explanation: 예상된 결과와 실제 반응의 대비, 그리고 회복력의 진짜 원인을 밝히는 패턴
examples: The opposition, far from disbanding under pressure, has reorganised into a decentralised network -- a resilience rooted not in charismatic leadership but in grassroots structures.
The currency, far from collapsing under sanctions, has stabilised -- a resilience rooted not in economic fundamentals but in capital controls and black-market dynamics.

drillQuestions.question: The IRGC has activated ______ plans to disperse into as many as 30,000 independent five-man cells.

sentences.notes: far from + 동명사가 기대를 뒤엎는 현실을 효과적으로 전달. not A but B 구조가 IRGC 회복력의 복잡한 내부 동학을 분석한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The IRGC, far from collapsing under the bombardment
koreanAlignment.ko: IRGC는 폭격 아래 무너지기는커녕

koreanAlignment.en: has activated contingency plans to disperse into 30,000 independent five-man cells
koreanAlignment.ko: 비상 계획을 가동하여 30,000개의 독립적 5인 조로 분산했다

koreanAlignment.en: a resilience rooted not in ideological cohesion
koreanAlignment.ko: 이념적 결속이 아닌 곳에 뿌리를 둔 회복력

koreanAlignment.en: a rank-and-file loyalty that extends more to Iran's territory than to the regime's survival
koreanAlignment.ko: 정권의 생존보다 이란 영토에 더 향하는 병사들의 충성심

sentences.original: The ultimate irony of Operation Epic Fury is that the officials who launched it -- J.D. Vance and his allies in Trump's orbit -- entered office arguing that America had squandered blood and treasure in post-2001 Middle Eastern wars and must husband its resources for a potential conflict with China, yet the war they have chosen to fight is cannibalising forces from Asia, diverting a marine expeditionary unit from Japan and THAAD components from South Korea, and degrading missile-defence capability in a manner that, as one analyst at CSIS warns, "may well undercut deterrence in the Pacific for the remainder of this decade."
sentences.translation: 에픽 퓨리 작전의 궁극적 아이러니는, 이를 개시한 관리들 -- J.D. 밴스와 트럼프 궤도의 동맹자들 -- 이 미국이 2001년 이후 중동 전쟁에서 피와 재물을 낭비했으며 중국과의 잠재적 분쟁을 위해 자원을 아껴야 한다고 주장하며 취임했다는 점이다. 그런데 그들이 선택한 이 전쟁은 아시아에서 전력을 잠식하고 있으며, 일본에서 해병 원정부대를, 한국에서 사드 구성품을 전용하고, CSIS의 한 분석가가 경고하듯 "이번 10년의 남은 기간 동안 태평양에서의 억제력을 약화시킬 수 있는" 방식으로 미사일 방어 능력을 저하시키고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The ultimate irony of Operation Epic Fury
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is that
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 보어절 접속사

grammarElements.text: the officials who launched it entered office arguing that
grammarElements.label: 보어절 내 주절

grammarElements.text: yet the war they have chosen to fight is cannibalising
grammarElements.label: 대조절 (yet)

vocabulary.example: The new product line cannibalised sales from the company's existing range.
Nuclear deterrence has been the foundation of strategic stability for decades.

expressions.usage: 전쟁에서의 인명 손실과 재정 소모를 비판적으로 표현할 때 사용하는 전통적 표현

patterns.pattern: The ultimate irony of [action] is that [those who launched it] entered office arguing [opposite position], yet [contradictory reality]
patterns.explanation: 행위자의 과거 주장과 현재 행동의 극적 모순을 드러내는 아이러니 패턴
examples: The ultimate irony of the spending bill is that its sponsors entered office promising fiscal discipline, yet the legislation adds more to the deficit than any they previously opposed.
The ultimate irony of the privacy law is that its architects entered office championing civil liberties, yet the statute grants surveillance powers they once condemned.

drillQuestions.question: The war they have chosen to fight is ______ forces from Asia, diverting a marine expeditionary unit from Japan.

sentences.notes: 기사의 마지막 문장이자 핵심 결론. 도입부의 아이러니를 더 구체적으로 완성하며 중국/태평양이라는 더 큰 전략적 맥락으로 확장한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The ultimate irony of Operation Epic Fury
koreanAlignment.ko: 에픽 퓨리 작전의 궁극적 아이러니

koreanAlignment.en: entered office arguing that America had squandered blood and treasure
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국이 피와 재물을 낭비했다고 주장하며 취임했다

koreanAlignment.en: the war is cannibalising forces from Asia
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 전쟁은 아시아에서 전력을 잠식하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: may well undercut deterrence in the Pacific
koreanAlignment.ko: 태평양에서의 억제력을 약화시킬 수 있다
